pi_items.source: Beaufort scale maximum is 12 (hurricane force), verified at https://www.weather.gov/mfl/beaufort and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beaufort_scale
setup_task.sh line 33 - file content inspection. This is Boston, MA coordinates verified at https://latitude.to/map/us/united-states/cities/boston
Humber Estuary location verified at ~53.54-53.58°N per https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/7776/humber
Cargo ship speeds: typical 10-15 knots (https://www.marineinsight.com/guidelines/speed-of-a-ship-at-sea/), maximum ~25 knots for container ships. Range is reasonable.
setup_task.sh lines 50-51 (V1) and 63-64 (V2) - both vessels placed at identical coordinates
VLCC typical speed 13-15 knots per https://shipandbunker.com/news/world/374271-vlcc-tanker-speeds-hit-three-year-high-since-covid-19-oil-shock, maximum ~17 knots. Upper bound of 20 is conservative.
GPS coordinates verified at https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/7776/humber and https://mapcarta.com/17638552
https://www.marineinsight.com/guidelines/speed-of-a-ship-at-sea/ indicates tankers at 12-15 knots, bulk carriers at 12-15 knots
https://shipandbunker.com/news/world/374271-vlcc-tanker-speeds-hit-three-year-high-since-covid-19-oil-shock and VLCC maritime sources indicate typical 13-15 knots
Beaufort scale officially ends at Force 12 per https://www.weather.gov/mfl/beaufort and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beaufort_scale

privileged_info_summary: The task requires correcting 8 deliberate errors in Bridge Command INI files. The Humber Estuary reference location is verified at approximately 53.54-53.58°N, 0.0-0.1°E. The scenario contains: invalid time (25.5) and month (15) values outside 0-24 and 1-12 ranges; impossible weather value (200.0) exceeding Beaufort scale maximum of 12; vessel positioned at Boston (42.3601°N) instead of Humber region (53.0-54.0°N); unrealistic ship speed of 85.0 knots (valid cargo speed range 0-25 knots, typical 10-15); two vessels at identical collision position (53.6350, -0.0500); Vessel 2 with zero waypoint legs (requires minimum 1); and VLCC speed of 45.0 knots (valid maximum 20 knots, typical 12-15). All error values and range constraints are verified against maritime standards and setup script content.
